text: Precision really matters to me — I want every word to be doing specific work, not just filling space. I write in a fairly formal academic register, so I tend to be careful about tone consistency throughout a section. I also think a lot about how ideas connect to each other, making sure there's a clear logical thread running through the argument rather than just a collection of points sitting next to each other. And since I'm usually writing for contexts where citations are expected, I try to leave the prose structured in a way that can absorb references naturally without the sentences becoming unwieldy.
